description: HubSpot CMS theme structure, configuration, and fields system
overview: A theme is a single directory of files including HTML, CSS, JavaScript, modules, and subdirectories. Two JSON files are mandatory at the root: theme.json and fields.json.

structure.recommended_structure:
my-theme/
├── theme.json
├── fields.json
├── templates/
│   ├── home.html
│   ├── page.html
│   ├── blog-listing.html
│   ├── blog-post.html
│   └── partials/
│       ├── header.html
│       └── footer.html
├── modules/
│   ├── hero.module/
│   ├── card.module/
│   └── cta.module/
├── css/
│   ├── main.css
│   └── theme-overrides.css
├── js/
│   └── main.js
├── images/
└── _locales/
    ├── en.json
    └── es.json

theme_json.description: Theme metadata file — required at root level

fields.description: Display name used throughout the HubSpot interface
Relative path to default template for theme preview
Image path showing theme appearance in selection screens
Version number with dot notation support
Theme author info: { name, email, url }
SPDX identifier or relative path to license file
Controls visibility in page creation UI

fields_json.description: Theme editor fields — define what the theme editor exposes to editors
fields_json.accessing_in_css: Theme values use {{ theme.path.to.value }} dot notation directly in CSS files

theme_css_usage.description: Using theme field values in CSS and templates
